title: Post-broadcast mark_note_spent is O(n) sequential awaits
description: After a successful broadcast in send_payment() (payment.rs:236–244), the code calls store.mark_note_spent(note_id, txid) in a for loop — one database await per selected note. With 100 input notes this is 100 round-trips to SQLite before the user sees confirmation. Fix: add WalletStore::mark_notes_spent(note_ids: &[i64], txid: &str) that issues a single UPDATE notes SET spent=1, spending_txid=? WHERE note_id IN (...) call. The per-note call path was probably written first for clarity; the bulk path is a mechanical replacement.
close_reason: Fixed: added mark_notes_spent() to WalletStore — wraps all updates in one SQLite transaction (one begin/commit instead of N individual awaits). payment.rs updated to use it. Single transaction eliminates per-note fsync overhead and reduces async overhead. Error handling preserved: one warn log on failure, scanner self-heals.
